segments.text: First listen and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: How many people visited the Great Exhibition of 1851?

translations.zh-Hans: 有多少人参观了1851年的万国工业博览会？

segments.text: Perhaps the most extraordinary building of the nineteenth century was the Crystal Palace, which was built in Hyde Park for the Great Exhibition of 1851?

translations.zh-Hans: 19世纪最非凡的建筑或许要数水晶宫；它为1851年的万国工业博览会而建于海德公园。

segments.text: The Crystal Palace was different from all other buildings in the world, for it was made of iron and glass.

translations.zh-Hans: 水晶宫不同于世界上其他所有建筑，因为它由钢铁和玻璃建成。

segments.text: It was one of the biggest buildings of all time and a lot of people from many countries came to see it.

translations.zh-Hans: 它是有史以来最大的建筑之一，许多国家的人纷纷前来参观。

segments.text: A great many goods were sent to the exhibition from various parts of the world.

translations.zh-Hans: 大量商品从世界各地运到博览会展出。

segments.text: There was also a great deal of machinery on display.

translations.zh-Hans: 现场还展出了大量机器。

segments.text: The most wonderful piece of machinery on show was Nasmyth's steam hammer.

translations.zh-Hans: 展品中最奇妙的机器是内斯密斯的蒸汽锤。

segments.text: Though in those days, traveling was not as easy as it is today, steamboats carried thousands of visitors across the channel from Europe.

translations.zh-Hans: 虽然当时旅行远不如今天方便，轮船仍从欧洲运送成千上万的游客横渡英吉利海峡。

segments.text: On arriving in England, they were taken to the Crystal Palace by train.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们一到英国，就由火车送往水晶宫。

segments.text: There were six million visitors in all, and the profits from the exhibition were used to build museums and colleges.

translations.zh-Hans: 参观者总计600万人，博览会的利润被用于兴建博物馆和高等院校。

segments.text: Later, the Crystal Palace was moved to South London.

translations.zh-Hans: 后来，水晶宫被迁到伦敦南部。

segments.text: It remained one of the most famous buildings in the world until it was burnt down in 1936.

translations.zh-Hans: 直到1936年毁于大火之前，它一直是世界上最著名的建筑之一。
